name: Shrimp Loaf
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb puff pastry, thawed or homemade
3 large tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.
2. Add the bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
3. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
4. Add the chopped tomatoes, stir to combine, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down into a paste, about 10 minutes.
5. Stir in the shrimp, salt, and pepper, and cook just until the shrimp are opaque, about 3-4 minutes.
6. In a separate bowl, mix together the flour and remaining olive oil to make a roux. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the roux is golden brown.
7. Remove the shrimp mixture from heat and let it cool slightly.
8. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the puff pastry into a rectangle about 12x16 inches.
9. Spread the cooled shrimp mixture in the center of the dough, leaving about an inch of dough around the edges.
10. Brush the edges of the dough with water to seal.
11. Fold the dough over the filling and press down to shape it like a loaf.
12. Place the shaped dough on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or aluminum foil.
13. Make two small slits in the top of the loaf to allow steam to escape during baking.
14. Brush the egg yolk evenly over the top of the pastry.
15. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 20 minutes, then reduce the temperature to 300°F (150°C) and continue baking until golden brown and cooked through, about 20-25 more minutes.
16. Serve hot.
